fix(pptx): expand regex backreferences in find/replace

Mirror the Word fix (commits 8096bd6b + e3f9fee6) for PPTX:
ProcessFindInPptParagraph concatenated the literal replace string when
find used regex (r"..." prefix or regex=true), so capture-group refs
like $1 / $2 / ${name} stayed unexpanded. Now we capture Match
objects from the original Regex.Matches pass and call Match.Result on
the corresponding match, which expands backrefs while preserving any
lookaround context.

Reproduce:
  set /sli

fix(word): preserve lookaround context when expanding regex backrefs

R28 fix re-ran Regex.Replace on the matched substring to expand backrefs,
which silently broke lookahead/lookbehind: a pattern like r"foo(?=bar)"
matches "foo" in fullText, but re-applying the pattern to just "foo"
fails (no following "bar"), so effectiveReplace fell back to the literal
substring and the document was reported "1 matched" but never modified.

Switch to capturing Match objects from the original Regex.Matches pas

chore: regenerate Cargo.lock for librefang-llm-drivers dep (#3318)

#3294 added `librefang-llm-drivers` as a path dep of `librefang-api`
in Cargo.toml but did not refresh Cargo.lock. The lock entry for
librefang-api was missing the line, so any `cargo build --locked`
(and tooling that asserts lockfile freshness) would fail. Local
non-locked builds silently regenerated the line on every run.

Regenerated via `cargo metadata`; diff is the single missing entry.

Issue: maintainer marks PR as needing changes (formal review or
"changes requested"-style comment), `needs-changes` label sticks
correctly. Author then pushes a new commit (or any random comment)
and `pr-labels.yml` flips the label back to `ready-for-review`
even though GitHub's `reviewDecision` is still `CHANGES_REQUESTED`
— observed on librefang/librefang#3262.

feat: support OPENCLAW_CONFIG_DIR env override for token sync path

Token sync wrote to \$HOME/.openclaw/workspace-<agent>/.secrets/linear.env,
which assumed the connector and openclaw share a host with the same user's
\$HOME. Doesn't hold when the connector runs as a different OS user, in a
container, or on a different host than the openclaw install.

OPENCLAW_CONFIG_DIR overrides the base path; falls back to \$HOME/.openclaw
when unset, preserving existing behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opu

fix: use deploy.resources for Synology kernel compatibility

Replace cpus/mem_limit/pids_limit with deploy.resources.limits syntax
to support Synology DSM kernels that don't support CPU CFS scheduler.

Synology error: 'NanoCPUs can not be set, as your kernel does not
support CPU CFS scheduler or the cgroup is not mounted'

The deploy.resources syntax is compatible with both modern Docker
and older Synology kernels.

fix: derive OAuth redirect_uri from request URL

Token exchange was hardcoded to /linear-webhook/callback, breaking the
flow whenever Linear authorized against a different path (e.g. /oauth/callback).
Now defaults to the actual request URL so the redirect_uri sent during
token exchange matches the one used during authorize.

Also enable Express's trust-proxy so req.protocol reflects X-Forwarded-Proto
when running behind a reverse proxy or tunnel (Cloudflare Tunnel, openresty,
etc.) — without thi

fix(word): throw on unclosed bracket in Query selector

ParseSingleSelector silently dropped malformed [attr=... selectors
because the inner regex required a closing ]. Direct API callers got
empty results with no error, while the CLI layer's AttributeFilter
already rejected the same input. Now ParseSingleSelector verifies
bracket balance and throws ArgumentException, aligning API and CLI
behavior.

fix(word): expand regex backreferences in find/replace

When find uses regex (r"..." prefix or regex=true), Set-with-replace
concatenated the literal replace string instead of expanding capture
group backrefs ($1, $2, ${name}). Now re-runs Regex.Replace on the
matched substring so backrefs resolve.

Reproduce: set /body/p[1] --prop 'find=r"(hello) (world)"' \
  --prop regex=true --prop 'replace=$2 $1'  →  "world hello"

discarded on the next rebuild — causing last_seen_ok to stay stale

fix(resident): detect file-vanished-mid-session on close, surface as error

If the resident-held document is unlinked from disk while the
resident is running, OpenXML keeps writing into the orphaned
inode on Dispose. Set/get/close all reported success but the
on-disk path stayed missing — silent data loss.

DoShutdownAsync now probes File.Exists after handler.Dispose
and flags _shutdownFileMissing. The __close__ ack returns
exit=1 + a clear stderr message in that case, which the close
command su

0.6.4: 3 data-integrity fixes from review

#9 ON-CONFLICT-edit duplicating attachments
  Telegram edited_message events re-fire recordInbound with the same
  (chat_id, msg_id). Telegram doesn't permit replacing media in an
  edit, so the attachment rows from the original message are still
  correct — re-inserting would duplicate them and reset
  download_status to 'pending', losing the local_path of files we
  already fetched. recordInbound now early-exits the attachment loop
  when the message

fix(ci): add loop guards to claude-fix workflow (#192)

The claude-fix workflow uses a GitHub App token (not GITHUB_TOKEN),
so its pushes trigger subsequent workflow runs — creating a loop:
push → lint → workflow_run → claude-fix → push → repeat.

Add two layers of protection:
1. All 3 workflow_run jobs now check head_commit.message for
   "Auto-fix:" prefix and skip if present (zero runner cost)
2. PR-Agent review job gets FIX_COUNT guard (max 1 per PR),
   matching the pattern already used by

Normalize instance lookup across runtime launch URL and railway detail routes to tolerate historical instance identifier shapes, and prevent opening raw Railway public URLs before an instance reaches RUNNING. This removes the noisy pending-state blank-page behavior while preserving backward compatibility for existing instance cards.

Constraint: Existing instance identifiers may appear as DB id, prefixed instance id, or service-derived value

install.sh: fix two real install-killers found on PEPKEY VPS

1. xAI key paste truncation (84 → 41 chars)

   read_masked() did per-char `read -rs -n1` for live `*` echo feedback
   on key paste. On long pastes over SSH the loop breaks early —
   bracketed-paste escape sequences (ESC[200~ … ESC[201~) and other
   terminal control bytes mid-paste make `read -n1` return an empty
   char, which the loop interpreted as "Enter pressed" and broke at
   the wrong byte. Result: 84-char xAI keys silently

fix(pptx): top-level animation= prop rejects contradictory class tokens

bt-1/fuzz-1: 'set --prop animation=fly-in-out' bypassed the R23
ParseEffectClassSuffix gate (which only intercepts effect=) and was
parsed by ApplyShapeAnimation's flat segment loop. Both 'in' and 'out'
were valid class tokens so presetClass got last-wins overwrite,
silently producing an exit animation when the user typed in-out (or an
entrance for out-in). Track the first class token seen and throw on
any subsequent segmen

1. send() now retries on 503 (BLOCKER)
   Previously a single shot. Python adapter retries up to 3x with backoff
   (wechat.py:200-254 + wechat.py:568-579) so a transient bridge blip
   surfaces as failure to the user only when a real ~3s outage occurs.
   New: SEND_MAX_ATTEMPTS=3, linear backoff with mild jitter; only retries
   on SEND_RETRYABLE_STA

fix(gateway): remove SIGTERM shutdown-flag check from run_gateway_turn

The shutdown flag is set by SIGTERM handler in HermesAgent::new(). In
poll_queue mode this correctly breaks the polling loop on shutdown. In
gateway mode, if the process receives SIGTERM but survives (because the
Slack WebSocket stays connected), subsequent messages were immediately
returned as "shutting down" without calling the LLM. A single gateway
turn should always complete — the gateway lifecycle is managed by the
Slac

the siclaw-inner re-deploy that motivated this PR — the helm command
returned 'deployed' but pods kept running the old build.

fix(pptx): animation Get returns trigger (onClick/afterPrevious/withPrevious)

PopulateAnimationNode was emitting effect/class/presetId/duration but
omitted the trigger that Add accepts, breaking canonical Add↔Get
roundtrip. Read the trigger from effectCTn.NodeType (ClickEffect,
AfterEffect, WithEffect) and surface it as Format["trigger"].

fix(pptx): animation effect suffix routes class (fly-out → exit)

Previously effect=fly-out was concatenated with default class=entrance to
build 'fly-out-entrance-500-click'. The parse loop saw 'out' first
(class→exit) but then 'entrance' overrode it back, producing a fly-in
animation instead of fly-out. Now AddAnimation strips trailing
-in/-out/-entrance/-exit/-emph(asis) from the effect name and uses it
as the default class (still overridable by an explicit class= prop).

fix(scaffold): give wechatBridgePlugin a valid ChannelPlugin shape

Codex review on M1 flagged the only blocker: the previous stub was
`{__status: 'scaffold'}`, which fails openclaw's
`normalizeRegisteredChannelPlugin` (src/plugins/channel-validation.ts:39-51)
because the `id` field is missing — the loader rejects the registration
on first attempt and the plugin never reaches the bundled-register
pipeline at channel-entry-contract.ts:480-490.

Fix: minimal-but-valid ChannelPlugin literal with id

fix(xlsx): scatter/bubble series rewrite x/yVal refs, never insert <c:cat>

ApplySeriesReferences was written for category-style series (<c:cat> +
<c:val>). For a scatter/bubble chart with dataRange or top-level
categories=Sheet1!..., the rewriter would (a) skip the values branch
(no <c:val> on a scatter series) and (b) append a fresh <c:cat> with a
strRef. CT_ScatterSer has no cat slot, so the resulting chart failed
OOXML schema validation.

For ScatterChartSeries / BubbleChartSeries, rewrite t

fix(pptx): route shape-level spc/lang/kern to first-run rPr

Drawing run-property attributes (spc, lang, altLang, kern, cap,
kumimoji, normalizeH, baseline, ...) at shape level previously fell
through SetRunOrShapeProperties' default branch when runContext=false.
The DrawingRunPropertyAttrs gate required runContext=true, so the value
landed in SetGenericAttribute(shape, key, value) and was written as an
attribute on <p:sp> — silently invalid; Office ignores it.

Drop the runContext gate on the r

fix(pptx): scope @id=/@name= lookup to enclosing group context

Previously ResolveIdPath always searched the slide-level shape tree for
every @id= segment, so /slide[1]/group[@id=10003]/shape[@id=10000] would
walk into group[N] but then look for shape[@id=10000] under the slide
shape tree and resolve it to the wrong positional index — leaving the
final positional path /slide[1]/group[N]/shape[K] pointing at a child
slot that doesn't exist (group only has 2 children).

Iterate the @id=/@name= mat

root-level paths. The backup manifest never included workspace/IDENTITY.md
so cross-host restore landed an unnamed agent. The agent's identity
was effectively lost on every restore.

fix(file-tools): escalate to BLOCKED on repeated read_file dedup stubs (#16382)

read_file's dedup path returned a lightweight stub on re-reads of an
unchanged file, then returned early — so the consecutive-read loop
guard (hard block at count>=4) at the bottom of read_file_tool never
ran for stub-looped calls. Weaker tool-following models (local Qwen3.6
variants in the reported case) ignore the passive 'refer to earlier
result' hint and hammer the same read_file call until iteration budget
runs

test(gateway): harden session event setup timeout

fix: scope pending eviction by (sessionKey, sessionId), not sessionKey alone

Chain state in chain.ts is keyed by (sessionKey, sessionId) — two sessions
sharing a sessionKey but with different sessionIds are distinct. The
previous fix only matched on sessionKey, so starting one such session
would still evict the other's stashed before_tool_call entries — the same
bug, just at a finer granularity.

Changes:
- PendingCall gains an optional sessionId; beforeToolCall stashes it from
  ctx.sessionId

fix: classify sessions_spawn and subagents as high risk

Both tools were mapped to `system.application.launch`, which the SDK
resolves as low risk. Spawning a new agent session is a high-privilege
operation — the spawned agent inherits tool access and acts on the
user's behalf — so receipts now use `system.command.execute` (high
risk), giving audit trails the right weight.

Closes #106.

still filtered by `path.extension() == json`, so it picked up old leftover
JSON backups (within retention window) and missed all new tar.gz files.

Changed to `name.ends_with(.tar.gz)` to match the new format.

fix: scope session_start pending eviction to current session only

pending.clear() in the session_start handler wiped stashed call data for
every in-flight tool call across all sessions, not just the one that was
starting. When two sessions ran concurrently, a new session_start could
silently discard before_tool_call stashes belonging to another session,
causing those receipts to lose their startedAt timestamp and fall back to
recomputing paramsHash from the event params instead of the original

Fix hchat duplicate delivery via exchange self-call deadlock

When handle_hchat_exchange received a message for a local agent,
it called send_hchat() which did a blocking urllib urlopen back to
the same aiohttp server. This deadlocked the event loop for 10s,
causing the sender to time out and fall back to Remote /hchat,
resulting in two copies of the same message delivered to the agent.

Fix: detect when to_instance == local instance and deliver directly
via await runtime.enqueue_api_text(), byp

Stop hook: only kill typing, keep progress + pending alive

CC fires Stop the moment the visible reply lands, but the agent
typically continues with background work after that — sub-agents
finishing, post-reply edits to identity files, file cleanup, etc. The
old handler cleared pendingChats and deleted the progress message on
Stop, so every one of those tool events came in with pending=[none]
and got skipped. The user saw the agent visibly stop showing activity
right after the first reply even t

feat(model): promote is_compaction and is_skill to typed fields

Step.is_compaction (bool|None) and ToolCall.is_skill (bool|None) become
typed first-class fields instead of polymorphic extra dict keys. This
gives the frontend a stable, type-safe contract for rendering
compaction boundaries and skill tool icons; the typed flags are set by
every parser that detects the corresponding signal (8 parsers for
compaction, 4 for skills).

fix(run-scan): build one CMD array — bash 3.2 trips on empty SEMANTIC_FLAG (#4)

OpenClaw's exec shell is bash 3.2 (macOS default). Expanding an empty
array under `set -u` ("${SEMANTIC_FLAG[@]}" with SEMANTIC_FLAG=())
fires "unbound variable" on bash 3.2 — bash 4.4+ silently allows it,
which is why the Ubuntu CI contract test passed but the agent failed
in chat with:

  scripts/run-scan.sh: line 57: SEMANTIC_FLAG[@]: unbound variable

fix: KB recall uses raw similarity instead of length-normalized score

Root cause: lengthNormalizeScore over-penalized long KB entries
- Raw vector similarity ~0.24 → lengthNorm ~0.09 → below 0.45 threshold
- Result: 0 recalls even for clearly relevant entries

Fix:
- KB recall now uses raw vector similarity (1 - distance) directly
- Threshold lowered to 0.18 (realistic for BGE embedding on Chinese queries)
- Active hint threshold: 0.8 → 0.5
- Dynamic 0-3 results still applies

codex: fix JSONB SQL escape so the message column round-trips

capture.ts and stop.ts ran sqlStr() over the JSON.stringify'd row,
which doubles backslashes. With standard_conforming_strings=on
that corrupts the \" sequences produced by the inner stringify of
tool_input/tool_response, so PostToolUse(Bash) inserts fail with
400 invalid input syntax for type json. Stop with a quoted
assistant message would also store mangled JSON.

fix(telegram): close connection after poll cycle to see auth flips

The _chats_conn connection was reused across poll cycles. When the
inline-keyboard callback runs (via callback_query), it calls
set_auth_status() which commits auth_status='allowed' to the DB.
But the next poll cycle's _is_authorized() call used the same
connection object, which had an open read transaction from before
the write—SQLite WAL isolation meant it saw stale 'pending' status.

Close the connection after processing each

feat: directional health diagnostics for federated peers (#3)

The current `healthy?: boolean` only captures one direction — "can I
reach them?" — so a one-way routing failure (e.g. their tunnel down,
ours up) looks identical to a full outage. This adds inferred-from-local
direction tracking so operators can immediately tell which side of the
peering broke.

New Peer fields (all optional):
- `healthState`: 'established' | 'degraded-outbound' | 'degraded-inbound' | 'down'
- `healthStateChangedAt`

v7: probe known claude CLI paths instead of relying on PATH

CRITICAL bug surfaced by validation: when Claude Code spawns a Stop
hook subprocess, it inherits a minimal PATH from its launcher. On macOS
this means /opt/homebrew/bin (where Homebrew installs `claude`) is
NOT in PATH for the spawned hook, even though the user has claude
installed and authenticated.

Symptom: log line `[skip:<sid>] auth=none reason=no_auth (operational)`
on a Mac that has the canonical Claude Code install.

fix(telegram): use DB auth_status gate for inbound chats

The poll loop short-circuited on the legacy env-allowlist
(`self.allowed`, frozen at startup) before consulting the DB-based
`auth_status`. Result: groups approved via the inline-keyboard
"Allow" button stayed blocked because their chat_id was never in
the env set.

Replace the raw set check with `_is_authorized(chat_id)`, which
already does the layered gate: env allowlist → operator DM →
DB `auth_status='allowed'`.

fix: sync-memory-md dedup logic - replace broken zero-vector search with 3-tier matching

Before: used zero-vector search + only checked first 50 chars + limit(10)

  2. Exact text match (after normalization)
  3. Substring match (>= 80 char overlap in either direction)
  4. N-gram Jaccard similar

fix: delay run-in-terminal dispatch when Activity Panel just opened

When the panel was closed and the user clicked "Run in Terminal", the
code called onToggleActivityRef() to open the panel and immediately
fired the run-in-terminal CustomEvent in the same tick. The panel's
useEffect listener hadn't mounted yet, so the event was lost — requiring
a second click to actually run the command.

Fix: capture whether the panel was closed before toggling, then delay
the dispatch 150ms so the panel's use

- Emit a zero_matches warning (stderr in plain mode

v5: YAML-escape title and session_id in frontmatter

Discovered via v4 write_failures.log: when Haiku returned a title
containing a colon, hash, quote, or other YAML-special character, the
generated frontmatter became unparseable and gbrain put exited 1 with
a YAML error. Page write was lost.

Fix: always double-quote title and session_id in frontmatter, escaping
backslashes, double-quotes, and newlines. This makes the frontmatter
robust against arbitrary string content.

perf(cache): stale-while-revalidate for disk cache (#412)

Hard refreshes after the in-memory cache TTL expires (or after a gateway
restart drops in-memory entries) were paying the full ~20s
openclaw recipes list subprocess again, even though a recent-but-expired
disk entry was sitting right there. SWR fixes this: an expired disk hit
now returns the stale value immediately and kicks off a background
subprocess that updates memory + disk for the next call.

OpenClaw skill that registers Voxrn as an outbound MCP server. Tool
catalog: call.place, call.list_active, call.end, transcript.stream,
message.send, contact.search, contact.upsert, balance.check.

installed via 'openclaw skills install voxrn', any OpenClaw-bridged
chat (Slack, Telegram, iMessage, WhatsApp, Discord) gets the same
tool catalog the Voxrn web app's agent dispatcher uses.

status-server: cron run/toggle/reschedule via openclaw CLI shell

Adds three new endpoints that route iOS cron actions through the
openclaw binary instead of editing jobs.json directly (which OpenClaw
overwrites from in-memory state, causing run/toggle/delete to bounce
back):

* POST /cron/<id>/run       → openclaw cron run <id>
* PATCH /cron/<id> {enabled}   → openclaw cron enable|disable <id>
* PATCH /cron/<id> {schedule}  → openclaw cron edit <id> --cron|--every

token exchange against npmjs.org and is silently rejected with a 404 by
the registry. Upgrade to npm@latest before `npm publish` so Trusted
Publishing actually authenticates.

fix(shell_hooks): parse hooks_auto_accept as strict bool/string, not bool() (#16322)

`_resolve_effective_accept()` used `return bool(cfg_val)` for the
`hooks_auto_accept` config key. In Python, `bool("false")` is `True`,
so a user setting `hooks_auto_accept: "false"` (quoted YAML string)
in `config.yaml` would silently enable auto-approval of every shell
hook, bypassing the consent prompt entirely.

Replace the coercion with the same type-aware parsing already used for
the HERMES_ACCEPT_HOOKS e

the old (broken) behaviour that prefixed with the config dir, so CI
flagged a left/right mism

sdk(python): brigade composer architecture (NL→SAL compiler)

Brigade is a multi-station compiler-style NL→SAL composer that replaces
keyword-stacking compose() as the primary path. Architecture:

- Parser (mise en place): NL → ParsedRequest IR (verb, dobj, targets,
  slots, conditions, schedule, modifiers, namespace hints)
- 26 namespace station experts (one per A-Z): pure functions, no shared
  state, propose FrameProposals for inputs in their namespace
- Orchestrator (head chef): picks best p

fix(web-ui): reset session state to idle after send message failure

When sending a message fails while the session is still busy (e.g.
PROCESSING state), the state was transitioned to ERROR but never reset
to IDLE. This caused the session to appear stuck in an error state:
- Subsequent DialogTurnCompleted events were ignored
- BACKEND_STREAM_COMPLETED and FINISHING_SETTLED transitions were skipped
- The UI showed a persistent 'stoppable' indicator even though the session
  had already finished

without the NVIDIA `nvapi-`

Add /chat + /cognitive Tailscale serve routes inline (fixes 404)

Fresh VPS installs were missing /chat + /cognitive Tailscale routes
because my `setup_tailscale_cognitive_routes()` ran BEFORE the main

guard `tailscale serve status >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 0` correctly
short-circuited (no serve config existed yet), but no follow-up call
re-tried after the OpenClaw serve was added later.

fix(copilot): always exchange OAuth token before fetching Copilot models

The Copilot /models endpoint requires a short-lived session token; calling
it with the long-lived OAuth token can return HTTP 200 with an empty
data array, which surfaced as the misleading "empty model list" warning.
Match the chat path's behaviour and exchange first, falling back to the
supplied token only if exchange fails (so imported session tokens still
work).

fix(agent): resolve relative media paths in MessageTool

When deployed with Docker and workspace mounted as a volume, sending
media files failed because relative paths (e.g. output/image.png) were
not resolved against the workspace directory. The process CWD differs
from the workspace in containerized environments, causing os.path.isfile
checks to fail in channel handlers. Normalize relative media paths at
the MessageTool entry point using get_workspace_path().

fix(config): preserve WebSocket buffer in non-proxy ws_connect_with_proxy path (#5794)

The non-proxy path in `ws_connect_with_proxy` called `connect_async`
then immediately `into_inner()` + `from_raw_socket` to normalize the
return type to `ProxiedWsStream`.  This discards data already buffered
by tungstenite's frame codec, causing channels that use WebSocket
(Slack Socket Mode, Discord, DingTalk, Lark, QQ) to silently miss
server frames — the connection succeeds but no events are ever
received

fix: signal compression boundary to context engine

When _compress_context rotates session_id (compression split), fire
on_session_start(new_sid, boundary_reason="compression",
old_session_id=<old>) on the active context engine. Plugin engines
(e.g. hermes-lcm) use this to preserve DAG lineage across the rollover
instead of re-initializing fresh per-session state.

Built-in ContextCompressor.on_session_start accepts **kwargs and ignores
them — no behavior change for default users.

fix(file-tools): broaden dedup-status write guard to cover small wrappers

The write_file guard added in #16223 used strict equality against the
internal dedup status message. In practice, the model sometimes
prepends a short note or appends a trailing comment before calling
write_file, which slipped past the strict check.

Broaden the heuristic: reject writes whose stripped content equals
the status message OR contains it and is <=2x its length. Short,
status-dominated writes are always corrupt

fix(update): use npm ci to stop rewriting package-lock on every update (#16295)

`npm install --silent` (used by `_build_web_ui` and `_update_node_dependencies`)
silently rewrites package-lock.json on npm ≥ 10 (strips "peer": true etc.),
leaving the working tree dirty after every `hermes update`. The next update
then detects the dirty lockfile and stashes it — producing a trail of
hermes-update-autostash entries for web/package-lock.json, ui-tui/package-lock.json,
and root package-lock.json.

UNIQUE constraint violation. Because advanceChain is called after the
insert, it is never reac

grpc-netty-shaded bundles its own Netty classes which would conflict with
standalone Netty deps needed for Unix domain socket (DomainSocketAddress).
Using grpc-netty (non-shaded) allows clean Unix socket support in T2.

Fix deploy_at CLI: network was passed as positional escrow_id arg

deploy_at() signature is deploy_at(passphrase, worker, deadline, preimage,
escrow_id=None, network=None). The CLI was calling it as:

    deploy_at(..., args.deadline_block, args.preimage_hex, args.network)

which put args.network into the escrow_id slot, naming every AT
"SIGmainnet" or "SIGtestnet" — causing name collisions and the AT
lookup to find the wrong contract.

Fixed to use network=args.network keyword argument.

fix(deploy): use systemctl restart on Linux to avoid Restart=on-failure gap

pkill sends SIGTERM which causes supervise to exit 0 (clean shutdown).
systemd's Restart=on-failure only triggers on non-zero exits, so the
agent stays dead after a `pkill`-based deploy.

Now uses `sudo systemctl restart acc-agent` on Linux when the unit is
active — systemd marks the unit for restart regardless of exit code.
Falls back to pkill if systemctl unavailable or unit inactive, then
uses `systemctl start` to ha

fix(pptx): crop / cropleft / croptop / cropright / cropbottom accept trailing % suffix

The error message already advertised 'Expected a percentage (0-100)', so
rejecting the literal '%' suffix was self-contradictory. Strip a trailing
'%' before parsing in both the Add and Set picture-crop paths.

fix(xlsx): conditionalformatting Add rejects unknown CF type to match cf alias

The cf alias rejected unknown type values (R9), but the conditionalformatting
alias still silently fell through to dataBar, hiding typos like type=invalid.
Both aliases now share the same allowlist.

fix(pptx): gradient parser tolerates single-color input at front-end

aaae88bf added a duplicate-on-empty fallback after angle/focus stripping,
but an earlier guard (parts.Length < 2) rejected single-color forms like
'gradient=FF0000' before the fallback could run. Relax that guard so the
existing back-end tolerance is reachable from CLI input.

fix(pptx): shape opacity rejects out-of-range values instead of writing invalid XML

a:alpha/@val must be in [0, 100000]. Negative input (e.g. opacity=-1)
previously emitted <a:alpha val="-100000"/>, corrupting the file.
Throw ArgumentException for inputs outside [0.0, 1.0] (after the >1
percentage normalization). Matches the table-cell opacity branch's
clamping intent and is consistent with the documented 0-100 range.

fix(core): IsTruthy strips BOM and zero-width chars before matching

R8's Trim() left the BOM (U+FEFF) and other zero-width / format characters
in place because they are not classified as whitespace. Inputs like
'\uFEFFtrue' threw ArgumentException instead of being treated as truthy.
Apply the same stripping to IsTruthySafe and IsValidBooleanString for
consistency.

fix(pptx): view svg --page honors page argument instead of always rendering slide 1

Both the local-process view path and the resident-server view path previously
ignored --page for SVG mode and used --start (line/paragraph) as the slide
index. HTML mode already honored --page; now SVG matches.

- ToolStreamManager: 80ms throttle batch updates, 50-entry FIFO eviction, 120K char truncation
- ChatEventHandler: sessionKey filtering to ignore other sessions' events
- SessionViewModel: updated ToolStreamManager instantiation with new params

Based on openclaw webchat patterns for optimal mobile streaming experience.

fix(xlsx): cf Add rejects unknown type instead of silently emitting dataBar

The AddCf dispatch fell through to the conditionalformatting (dataBar)
branch for any unrecognized type, so typos like type=badtype produced

fix(xlsx): chart layout props reject NaN/Infinity instead of writing invalid XML

double.TryParse accepts "NaN" / "Infinity" and the resulting
<c:x val="NaN"/> XML is unparseable by Excel. Add !double.IsFinite guard
to the five manual-layout cases (plotarea, title, legend, trendlinelabel,
displayunitslabel) so non-finite values land in the unsupported list
instead of corrupting the chart part.

fix(xlsx): slicer caption strips XML invalid chars to avoid Dispose crash

Caption values containing XML 1.0 illegal characters (\x00-\x08, \x0B-\x0C,
\x0E-\x1F) round-tripped through the OOXML DOM but threw ArgumentException
during serialization on Dispose. Filter via XmlConvert.IsXmlChar at assign
time so Add succeeds and the file closes cleanly.

caused every agent comment containing a bare issue identifier to fail CLI-wide.

Invalid underline tokens (e.g. 'zigzag') now raise:

matching the alignment/tab-val/leader pattern.

Previously fell through to OpenXML SDK enum constructor and leaked
'The specified value is not valid according to the specified enum
type. (Parameter "value")' which exposes internal API surface.

fix: validate persistent cli gateway readiness

Hardens setup and wrapper flows by recreating stale compose definitions, verifying all three containers are running, and checking gateway /readyz from inside openclaw-cli before executing admin commands.

Add ACClient.stream() and adopt in three single-pass scripts

stream() is a generator-based pagination helper that yields records
one at a time. Memory peak is O(page size) instead of O(total).
paginate() is now a thin wrapper: return list(self.stream(...)).
Existing callers see no change.

Single-pass adoptions:
- role_address_finder: aggregates matches as it reads. Bounded by
  match count (typically <1k records).
- free_vs_corporate_report: tallies by domain. Bounded by unique
  domain count

fix(word): Add accepts contextualSpacing prop on paragraph

Set already supported contextualSpacing on paragraphs (Set.cs:529),
but Add reported it as UNSUPPORTED via schema gating (add:false) and
had no handler branch. Restores Add/Set symmetry per the project's
Feature Implementation Checklist — schema flips to add:true and
AddParagraph mirrors the widowControl truthy/false branch.

fix(word): Get returns all per-script font slots on run/paragraph

paragraph/run Get previously collapsed RunFonts to a single "font" key
(Ascii first), dropping eastAsia/hAnsi/cs slots even when Set wrote them
correctly. Style Get already emitted font.ascii/eastAsia/hAnsi/cs as
canonical keys (Query.cs:546-553); run/paragraph now mirror the same
shape with no flat "font" alias. Effective.* fallback updated to check
slot keys instead of legacy "font".
